design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platformer levels as Jerry, avoiding obstacles and collecting items (cheese bits, hearts) while progressing through themed environments (Theater, Junk Yard, Toy Store, House)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Standard platformer challenge: precise jumping and timing to avoid hazards and enemy patterns
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Jerry Mouse navigating domestic and industrial environments, evading threats and collecting resources
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Generic licensed platformer with minimal narrative integration of the Tom & Jerry property; gameplay appears disconnected from franchise mechanics (no Tom-Jerry cat-mouse dynamic in core loop)

difficulty.lives_system: Lives-based system with 99 lives cheat available, suggesting standard SNES platformer health model
difficulty.continues_system: Japan version includes continue option on Game Over screen; US/EU versions removed this feature, suggesting regional difficulty balancing
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression with stage select system (7 themed environments with multiple stages per location)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Moderate agency: cheat codes available for stage skip, 99 lives, 99 hearts, and infinite mid-air jumps; disabled cheats suggest difficulty tuning concerns during development

progression.upgrade_system: Health collection (hearts) and resource accumulation (cheese bits) for stage warp progression; cheese bits tied to hidden stage select cheat
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential stage progression through Theater (3 stages) → Junk Yard (3 stages) → Toy Store (3 stages) → House (2 stages + Boss 4) → Ending
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select cheat requiring specific cheese bit collection
Multiple difficulty paths (continue availability differs by region)

world_building_techniques: Themed level environments (Theater, Junk Yard, Toy Store, House) that reference Tom & Jerry cartoons
Implicit setting through background art and obstacle design

danger_signals: Regional version differences (continue system removal in US/EU) indicate aggressive difficulty localization
environmental_storytelling: Themed locations (Theater, Junk Yard, Toy Store, House) establish implicit narrative context
Obstacle design contextual to environment setting

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Tom & Jerry SNES article)
